course_info.title: Digital Communications Capstone
course_info.meeting_schedule: Monday, Wednesday, Friday - 4 hours per week

class_schedule.topic: Capstone Kickoff: Client Projects and Team Formation
learning_objectives: Understand the capstone project scope, client expectations, and evaluation criteria
Form teams based on complementary skills in strategy, content, analytics, and design
Begin client research and initial stakeholder interviews
readings: Selected capstone project proposals from client organizations (provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: The capstone project is your opportunity to demonstrate everything you have learned across the Digital Communications program by solving a real communications challenge for a real client. Over 15 weeks, your team will develop, present, and begin executing a comprehensive communications strategy.
key_concepts: Client management: setting expectations, managing scope, professional communication protocols
Team formation: balancing strategic thinkers, content creators, data analysts, and designers
Project scoping: fitting ambition to timeline, team capability, and client budget constraints
Deliverable milestones: research, strategy, content plan, prototype content, measurement framework, final presentation
Professional conduct: NDA compliance, meeting management, progress reporting
lecture_notes.demonstration: Client project briefings: each client organization presents their communications challenge, followed by Q&A. Teams then form around projects based on interest and skill complementarity.
setup_requirements: Project management tool configured (Notion, Asana, or Trello)
Team communication channel established (Slack or Discord)
Client contact information and NDA signed

review.value: Defining exactly what the team will deliver, what is out of scope, and how scope changes are handled; the most common capstone failure is overscoping and delivering nothing well rather than delivering a focused project excellently
Professional-quality deliverables, regular status updates, receptiveness to feedback, and honest communication about challenges; the client relationship is part of the evaluation

learning_objectives: Present comprehensive research on the client's industry, competitive landscape, and current communications
Deliver a situation analysis with SWOT, audience insights, and strategic opportunities
Receive and incorporate faculty and client feedback on research quality
readings: Smith, Strategic Planning for Public Relations, Ch. 2-4 (review)

lecture_notes.introduction: Research is the foundation of strategy. A situation analysis built on assumptions rather than evidence produces strategies that feel right but fail in practice. This milestone ensures your strategy will be grounded in reality.
key_concepts: Competitive communications audit: how competitors communicate, what works, and where gaps exist
Audience research: surveys, interviews, analytics data, and secondary sources to understand real audience behavior
Content audit: evaluating the client's existing communications across all channels
Stakeholder interviews: structured conversations with client team members to understand internal perspectives
Research synthesis: distilling findings into strategic insights that point toward clear opportunities
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents their research and situation analysis (15 minutes), followed by structured critique from faculty and the client representative, using a standardized feedback form
setup_requirements: Completed research deliverables uploaded before class

review.value: Research must be current (within 12 months), sourced (every claim attributed), and actionable (every finding connected to a strategic implication); a research report that does not directly inform strategy is an academic exercise, not a capstone deliverable
Professional reception of client feedback: document all feedback, clarify ambiguities, distinguish between mandatory changes and suggestions, and communicate how feedback will be incorporated

learning_objectives: Present a complete strategic communications plan with objectives, audiences, channels, and content strategy
Defend strategic recommendations with research evidence and projected outcomes
Incorporate feedback for refined execution planning
readings: Completed strategy from team planning sessions
Client organization's strategic plan and annual report

lecture_notes.introduction: The strategic plan is the bridge between your research and your execution. It must be specific enough to guide daily decisions and compelling enough to earn client buy-in. Today you present your strategic recommendations and defend them against scrutiny.
key_concepts: Strategy presentation: situation summary, objectives, audience strategy, channel plan, content pillars, measurement
Budget justification: explaining why each recommended investment is worth the cost
Risk assessment: identifying what could go wrong and how you would respond
Phased implementation: breaking the strategy into immediate, near-term, and long-term actions
Client buy-in: earning commitment through clear rationale and projected outcomes
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents their strategic plan (20 minutes) followed by a 10-minute Q&A from faculty and client, simulating a real client strategy presentation
setup_requirements: Strategic plan document finalized and submitted before class

review.value: The ability to explain why you chose this approach over alternatives, what evidence supports the recommendation, what outcomes you project, and how you would measure success; a strategy you cannot defend is a strategy you do not fully understand

Breaking a comprehensive strategy into phases: quick wins (first 30 days), foundation building (60-90 days), and sustained growth (90+ days); demonstrating that the strategy is executable, not just aspirational

learning_objectives: Demonstrate prototype content across key channels meeting professional quality standards
Present initial analytics and performance data from any published content
Adjust content strategy based on early performance data and client feedback
readings: Team execution logs and analytics reports
Client feedback on initial content drafts

lecture_notes.introduction: Strategy without execution is fantasy. This milestone proves you can translate your strategic plan into real content that meets professional standards, reaches the intended audience, and generates measurable results.
key_concepts: Content prototyping: creating representative samples of each content type in the strategy
Quality control: editorial review, brand consistency checks, and technical quality standards
Performance tracking: establishing baselines and monitoring initial content performance
Iteration based on data: adjusting content based on what early metrics reveal
Client review workflow: managing content approval efficiently and professionally
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents their content prototypes and any early performance data (15 minutes), followed by quality review and strategic alignment feedback from faculty and client
setup_requirements: Content prototypes completed and ready for review
Analytics tracking configured for all published content

review.value: Content prototypes must be publication-ready, not rough drafts; they demonstrate to the client and faculty that the team can execute at the level the strategy requires
Showing that you can create content, measure its performance, learn from the data, and improve the next piece; this cycle is the core competency the capstone evaluates

class_schedule.topic: Milestone 4: Full Campaign Review and Optimization
learning_objectives: Present comprehensive campaign performance data with analysis and insights
Demonstrate optimization decisions made based on performance data
readings: Complete analytics reports from all channels

lecture_notes.introduction: This milestone evaluates not just what you produced but how you managed, measured, and improved the campaign over time. The ability to show a learning curve backed by data is as valuable as the content itself.
key_concepts: Performance narrative: telling the story of the campaign through data, from launch through optimization
Optimization documentation: showing what you changed, why, and what impact it had
Client deliverable preparation: packaging everything for client handoff in a professional, usable format
Knowledge transfer: ensuring the client can continue and evolve the work after your involvement ends
Team retrospective: honest assessment of what worked and what you would do differently
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents campaign performance, optimization decisions, and client-ready deliverables (20 minutes), followed by Q&A focused on learning and improvement
setup_requirements: Complete analytics dashboard with full campaign data
Optimization log documenting all changes and their impact

review.value: A documented record of every strategic or tactical change made during the campaign, including the data that triggered the change, the hypothesis behind the adjustment, and the measured result; proves that the team practiced data-driven decision-making

A complete set of materials the client needs to continue the work independently: strategy document, brand guidelines applied, content templates, editorial calendar, analytics dashboards, and recommended next steps

class_schedule.topic: Milestone 5: Final Client Presentation and Retrospective
learning_objectives: Deliver a polished final presentation to the client summarizing the full engagement
Present a comprehensive measurement report connecting activities to outcomes
Conduct an honest team retrospective analyzing the development process and personal growth
readings: Selected professional case studies in client presentation format

lecture_notes.introduction: The final presentation is your professional showcase. It synthesizes months of work into a compelling narrative of research, strategy, execution, and results. This is the deliverable you will reference in job interviews and portfolio presentations for years to come.
key_concepts: Final presentation structure: engagement overview, research findings, strategy, execution highlights, results, recommendations
Results framing: connecting communications metrics to the client's business objectives
Honest assessment: acknowledging what did not work alongside what did, with learning articulated
Next steps: actionable recommendations for the client's continued communications evolution
Portfolio presentation: framing the project for future employers and professional networking
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team delivers their final presentation to the client, faculty, and invited industry guests (25 minutes), followed by Q&A and formal feedback
setup_requirements: Final presentation materials polished and rehearsed
Individual reflection essays submitted before class

review.value: Presenting campaign results as a story: where we started (baseline), what we did (strategy and execution), what happened (metrics and outcomes), and what we learned (insights and recommendations); data without narrative is a spreadsheet, not a presentation
An honest assessment of team dynamics, strategic decisions, execution challenges, and personal growth; hiring managers value self-awareness and learning ability over claims of flawless execution

major_assignments.title: Milestone 1: Research and Situation Analysis
major_assignments.description: Deliver a comprehensive research package including competitive communications audit, audience research, client content audit, and stakeholder interview summaries, synthesized into a situation analysis with strategic implications.
objectives: Conduct thorough competitive and audience research
Audit the client's current communications across all channels
Synthesize research into actionable strategic insights
Present findings professionally to client and faculty
deliverables: Research report (12-15 pages) with all components
Competitive audit spreadsheet with channel-by-channel comparison
Audience research summary with persona drafts
Situation analysis with SWOT and strategic implications

criteria: Competitive audit is comprehensive and insightful
Audience research uses multiple methods and sources
Client content audit is systematic and evidence-based
Stakeholder insights add genuine internal perspective
Findings are synthesized into clear strategic implications
SWOT analysis identifies genuine strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats
Strategic priorities are justified by research evidence
Analysis demonstrates critical thinking beyond surface-level observation
Presentation is clear, professional, and within time limit
Key findings are communicated effectively
Team responds thoughtfully to questions
Client feedback is received and documented professionally
Comprehensive research demonstrating thorough investigative work
Insightful synthesis connecting findings to strategic implications
Professional presentation earning client confidence
Team collaboration evidenced throughout

major_assignments.title: Milestone 5: Final Client Deliverable and Presentation
major_assignments.description: Deliver the complete client engagement package including final strategy document, all content produced, analytics report with ROI analysis, client handoff materials, and a polished final presentation. Includes individual reflection on personal contribution and growth.
objectives: Deliver a professional-quality client engagement package
Present comprehensive results connecting activities to business outcomes
Provide the client with everything needed to continue the work independently
Reflect individually on contributions, challenges, and professional growth
deliverables: Final strategy document updated with execution results and recommendations
Complete content portfolio with all assets produced during the engagement
Analytics report with channel performance, optimization history, and ROI analysis
Client handoff package with templates, guidelines, calendar, and next steps
25-minute final presentation to client, faculty, and industry guests
Individual reflection essay (3-4 pages) on personal contribution and growth

criteria: Strategy document is comprehensive and updated with results
Content portfolio demonstrates professional production quality
Analytics report connects metrics to business outcomes with genuine insight
Handoff package enables client independence
Presentation is polished, compelling, and within time limit
Results narrative connects activities to outcomes effectively
Honest assessment of challenges alongside successes
Q&A responses demonstrate deep understanding of the engagement
Deliverables provide genuine strategic value to the client
Content produced is usable and aligned with client brand
Recommendations are actionable and grounded in data
Client relationship was maintained professionally throughout
Honest assessment of personal contribution with specific examples
Challenges overcome and skills developed are articulated clearly
Connection between capstone experience and career goals is thoughtful
Self-awareness about strengths and growth areas is genuine
Professional client deliverable package of portfolio quality
Compelling final presentation demonstrating strategic mastery
Genuine client impact evidenced by deliverable quality and results
Thoughtful individual reflection connecting experience to professional development

milestone_deliverables.description: Five milestone submissions tracking project progress and quality

final_deliverable.description: Quality and completeness of the final client engagement package

client_evaluation.description: Client assessment of professionalism, communication, and deliverable quality

peer_evaluation.description: Team contribution assessment from all team members

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all milestone reviews, client meetings, and the final presentation. Missing a milestone review without prior arrangement results in zero for that milestone.
course_policies.late_submissions: Milestone deadlines are firm. Late submissions are penalized 15% per day because subsequent milestones depend on timely delivery. The final presentation deadline is absolute.
course_policies.collaboration: This is a required team project. All team members must contribute substantively to every milestone. Peer evaluations adjust individual grades. Free-riding results in grade reduction.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All content, research, and analysis must be original team work. Client confidential information must be handled per NDA. Using work from other courses is self-plagiarism.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Teams must maintain shared project management tools, analytics dashboards, and content creation platforms. All deliverables must be professionally formatted and presentation-ready.
